name: Kaustubha Gem
description: The Kaustubha is a divine gem or jewel mentioned in Hindu scriptures. It originated during the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the cosmic ocean by the Devas (gods) and Asuras (demons) to obtain Amrita (the nectar of immortality). Many precious items emerged during this churning, including the Kaustubha. This gem is considered one of the most valuable ratnas (jewels) and is traditionally worn by Lord Vishnu on his chest, near his heart. It symbolizes purity, brilliance, and divine consciousness. The gem's radiance is said to dispel darkness and negativity, bringing prosperity and good fortune. It represents the divine light residing within all beings and Vishnu's role as the preserver of the universe. Some accounts associate the gem with Vishnu's consort, Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth and prosperity, further emphasizing its auspicious nature. The Kaustubha is not merely a beautiful object, but a symbol of divine grace and the triumph of good over evil, representing the ultimate reality and the eternal nature of Vishnu.
subtitle: The Jewel of Vishnu's Heart
origin: Churning of the Ocean of Milk (Samudra Manthana)
currentLocation: Adorns the chest of Lord Vishnu

keyMyths.title: Samudra Manthana
keyMyths.description: The Kaustubha Gem emerged during the churning of the ocean of milk, a collaborative effort between gods and demons to obtain Amrita.
keyMyths.source: Various Puranas, including the Bhagavata Purana

keyMyths.title: Vishnu's Adornment
keyMyths.description: Lord Vishnu wears the Kaustubha Gem on his chest, symbolizing his divine nature and his role as the preserver of the universe.

sources.author: Vettam Mani
